name: Dismiss the plausible-but-broken chain (precision + no invented impact)
context: IAM: access key AKIA-REPORT3 (leaked, see finding key-in-lambda) authenticates as role 'reporting'.
IAM: role 'reporting' policy allows s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::acme-invoices/*.
Data: bucket acme-invoices is tagged data-classification=financial (customer billing records).
IAM: access key AKIA-ANALYTICS9 (leaked, see finding key-in-terraform) authenticates as role 'analytics'.
IAM: role 'analytics' identity policy allows sts:AssumeRole on role 'data-admin'.
IAM: role 'data-admin' policy allows s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::acme-pii-lake/* (customer PII).

IAM: role 'analytics' has a permission boundary with an EXPLICIT Deny on sts:AssumeRole across the org — an explicit Deny overrides any Allow, so at policy evaluation the AssumeRole to data-admin does NOT resolve.

Network: host i-adminpanel security group allows its app port 8443 ONLY from the VPN CIDR 10.9.0.0/24. There is no internet route to i-adminpanel and no leaked credential in this estate grants VPN access.
Network: the customer database is reachable only from i-adminpanel's subnet (which is itself only VPN-reachable, per above).
Data: bucket acme-brochures is tagged data-classification=public.

findings.title: Access key AKIA-REPORT3 in a Lambda env var
findings.detail: a long-lived AWS access key is set as a plaintext Lambda environment variable in the repo

findings.title: Access key AKIA-ANALYTICS9 committed in main.tf
findings.detail: a long-lived AWS access key for role 'analytics' is hardcoded in the Terraform

findings.title: Critical unauth RCE on the internal admin panel that fronts the customer database
findings.detail: remote code execution on i-adminpanel, the admin panel in front of the customer database

findings.title: Bucket acme-brochures is world-readable
findings.detail: public ACL on acme-brochures, which holds only public marketing brochures
